title: pultenaea scabra var. montana
intro: Pultenaea scabra var. montana, also known as the mountain bush-pea, is a shrub in the family Fabaceae native to southeastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pultenaea scabra var. montana include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused oil to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness in modern medicine.
